author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
e mana! tumi se kevala bhūta
kusaṅga⋅śmaśāne, satata basicha,
pāiyā parama juta

mala mūtra jata, asata pacāla,
e tora bhakṣaṇa sukhe
‘rāma kṛṣṇa hari’, ‘gopāla govinda’,
balite nāricha mukhe

je ‘kara’ tomāra, govinda⋅pūjane,
tīratha bhramite pā⋅ya
se dui rākhile, curiye dāriye,
tabe ki ulṭā naya

jata nā karicha, sādhura helana,
se tora anala mukhe
dekha nā tāhāte, āpani dahicha,
emati goṅābi dukhe

kṛṣṇera vasati, sādhura hṛdaye,
sukhera viśrāma⋅bhūmi
emana durdaiva, tā̐hāra paraśa,
karite nāricha tumi

śrī⋅hari⋅caraṇa, karaha śaraṇa,
gayā⋅gaṅgā saba tā’te
kahe premānanda, tabe se uddhāra,
nahile vā habe kā’te
en_translation:
O my mind! You are merely a ghost, always sitting in the cremation ground of bad company, finding it very suitable there.

All impure and rotten things are your food for pleasure. You cannot even utter the names "Rāma," "Kṛṣṇa," "Hari," "Gopāla," or "Govinda."

Your hands are meant for worshipping Lord Govinda, and your feet are meant for traveling to holy places—if you keep them idle, stealing away their true purpose, then is that not utterly wrong?

All the disrespect you show to the saintly is like fire in your mouth. See how you are burning in it yourself, and thus you will pass your life in misery.

Lord Kṛṣṇa's abode is in the hearts of the saintly—a resting place of true happiness. Such is your misfortune that you are unable to obtain their touch.

Take refuge at the feet of Lord Hari; all holy places like Gayā and the Ganges are contained therein. Premānanda says: "Only then will you be delivered; otherwise, who will achieve it?"
